danger_signals.visual_form: box sprite positioned adjacent to wall sprite
danger_signals.meaning: imminent deadlock risk—box cannot be pulled back, only pushable forward

danger_signals.visual_form: box sprite in corner formed by two wall sprites
danger_signals.meaning: critical deadlock state—box permanently trapped if not on storage location

danger_signals.visual_form: box sprite in enclosed space with single entry point
danger_signals.meaning: severe constraint—box can only exit via the entry direction, limiting solution paths

danger_signals.visual_form: player sprite on wrong side of unsolved box
danger_signals.meaning: tactical danger—player cannot push box toward target without repositioning, consuming moves

danger_signals.visual_form: two or more box sprites adjacent to each other
danger_signals.meaning: deadlock vulnerability—pushing one box may trap another against wall or third box

safety_signals.visual_form: box sprite overlaid on or aligned with red dot marker
safety_signals.meaning: objective achieved for this box—one step closer to puzzle completion

safety_signals.visual_form: red dot markers on unoccupied floor squares
safety_signals.meaning: available destinations—paths remain open for box placement

safety_signals.visual_form: empty floor squares with no walls, boxes, or obstacles
safety_signals.meaning: maneuvering room—player has movement freedom to reposition for pushing

safety_signals.signal: Player in center of open area
safety_signals.visual_form: player sprite surrounded by empty floor squares
safety_signals.meaning: tactical safety—player has multiple directional options without immediate constraints

safety_signals.visual_form: box sprite with clear space on multiple sides
safety_signals.meaning: pushable in multiple directions—flexible solution pathways remain available

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: boxes not aligned with storage location markers; red dots visible beneath or near boxes
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: player can move and push boxes; input remains responsive; level progression blocked

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: all boxes positioned on storage locations; red dot markers fully obscured or merged with box sprites
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: level completion triggered; player typically advances to next puzzle or sees completion feedback

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: box permanently trapped against wall or in corner with no solution path remaining
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: puzzle becomes unsolvable; undo function activated or level restart required

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: some boxes on storage locations, others scattered; mix of solved and unsolved targets visible
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: player continues active pushing; partial visual feedback for completed objectives

environmental_storytelling.example: top-down layout with walls representing warehouse shelving and cargo areas; floor tiles suggest concrete or warehouse flooring

environmental_storytelling.technique: visual hierarchy through box vs. target contrast
environmental_storytelling.example: distinct visual separation between pushable boxes and storage location markers (dots) communicates goal without text

environmental_storytelling.technique: spatial constraint as narrative of logistics challenge
environmental_storytelling.example: narrow corridors, tight corners, and wall layouts implicitly communicate warehouse congestion and the difficulty of cargo handling

environmental_storytelling.technique: progressive level complexity as narrative escalation
environmental_storytelling.example: early levels feature open layouts; later levels introduce tighter spaces and more boxes, suggesting increasing operational difficulty

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: remains static until pushed by player; responds to directional force only; cannot be pulled; stops against obstacles
visually anchored to location; no further interaction needed; may display visual confirmation (color change, overlay)
moves one square at a time in four cardinal directions; can approach and push boxes; blocked by walls and occupied squares
movement limited by adjacent box positions; forced to navigate around unsolved boxes to access other areas
immovable barrier; blocks both player and box movement; defines level boundaries and internal architecture

notes: Sokoban contains no faction systems, combat states, or political dynamics. World-state communication is purely logical and spatial: danger signals manifest through geometric constraint (deadlock risk), while safety signals reflect solution availability. The game's entire manifestation layer operates on puzzle-logic visualization—red dots for goals, box positioning for risk assessment, and empty space for tactical freedom. NES version uses simple tile-based graphics; danger and safety are communicated implicitly through spatial relationships rather than explicit visual styling (no color-coded warnings or animated alerts).
